note: Install profiles — what a FRESH site starts as. flowwink.sh /update-funcs asks for one when site_settings.modules does not exist yet (nobody has logged in to /admin/modules at install time), writes the row, and deploys core + that profile's functions. Without this the selective-deploy filter fails open and tries to deploy every function — which exceeds the Supabase Free cap of 100 and fails silently partway through. Modules absent from a profile are written as {enabled:false} so the filter can skip their functions; the frontend merges this partial row with defaultModulesSettings (useModules.tsx), so name/description/structural fields still come from code.
generated_by: hand-maintained; module ids must exist in defaultModulesSettings (src/hooks/useModules.tsx) and function counts come from edge-function-map.json

cms.label: CMS — website, content and audience
cms.description: Pages, blog, media, forms, newsletter and surveys. The visual layer plus content capture. Smallest footprint.

crm.label: CRM — CMS plus customers, sales and support
crm.description: Everything in CMS, plus leads, companies, the 360° customer view, sales intelligence, bookings, calendar and live support.

erp.label: ERP — the full business operating system
erp.description: Everything in CRM, plus commerce, invoicing, quotes, subscriptions, accounting, HR and the rest. Exceeds the Supabase Free function cap — needs a paid plan, or drop modules after install.
